ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 (OilRig / Helix Kitten) DNS-tunnel C2 — Helminth / POWRUNER / Saitama-style beacon
ucs.description: Hunts the signature OilRig tradecraft: a PowerShell or scripting host generating high-entropy, abnormally long DNS labels (and frequent TXT lookups) to encode C2 traffic, as seen in Helminth, POWRUNER, BONDUPDATER, ALMA Communicator, DNSpionage, Glimpse, Karkoff and Saitama. Catches the actor's preferred channel rather than any one binary.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count, dc(DNS.query) as unique_queries, values(DNS.query) as queries, values(DNS.record_type) as record_types, count(eval(match(DNS.record_type,"TXT"))) as txt_count from datamodel=Network_Resolution where DNS.message_type="QUERY" by _time, DNS.src, DNS.process_name span=10m | rename DNS.* as * | eval avg_label_len=mvmap(queries, len(replace(query,"^[^.]+\.",""))) | eval long_label_count=mvcount(mvfilter(match(queries,"^[A-Za-z0-9+/=_\-]{30,}\."))) | where long_label_count>=5 AND (txt_count>=3 OR unique_queries>=20) AND NOT match(process_name,"(?i)(chrome|msedge|firefox|brave|opera|outlook|teams|svchost|nslookup|ping)\.exe$") | join type=outer src process_name [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.dest as src Processes.process_name as process_name Processes.process as cmdline Processes.parent_process_name as parent Processes.user as user] | table _time src user process_name parent cmdline long_label_count txt_count unique_queries queries
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let MinLongLabels = 5;
let ScriptingHosts = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend QName = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName))
| extend QType = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryType)
| where isnotempty(QName)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ScriptingHosts)
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
| extend FirstLabel = tostring(split(QName, ".")[0])
| extend FirstLabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel)
| extend LooksEncoded = FirstLabel matches regex @"^[a-z0-9+/=_\-]{30,}$"
| summarize
    LongLabelQueries = countif(LooksEncoded or FirstLabelLen >= 30),
    TxtQueries       = countif(QType =~ "TXT"),
    UniqueLabels     = dcount(FirstLabel),
    SampleNames      = make_set(QName, 8),
    SampleCmd        = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
    FirstSeen        = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen         = max(Timestamp)
  by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where LongLabelQueries >= MinLongLabels and (TxtQueries >= 3 or UniqueLabels >= 20)
| order by LongLabelQueries desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let MinLongLabels = 5;
let ScriptingHosts = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
ImDnsActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where isnotempty(DnsQuery)
| extend QName = tolower(DnsQuery)
| extend FirstLabel = tostring(split(QName, ".")[0])
| extend FirstLabelLen = strlen(FirstLabel)
| extend LooksEncoded = FirstLabel matches regex @"^[a-z0-9+/=_\-]{30,}$"
| summarize
    LongLabelQueries = countif(LooksEncoded or FirstLabelLen >= 30),
    TxtQueries       = countif(DnsQueryTypeName =~ "TXT"),
    UniqueLabels     = dcount(FirstLabel),
    SampleNames      = make_set(QName, 8),
    FirstSeen        = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen         = max(TimeGenerated)
  by DvcHostname, SrcIpAddr, SrcUsername
| where LongLabelQueries >= MinLongLabels and (TxtQueries >= 3 or UniqueLabels >= 20)
| join kind=leftouter (
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where TargetProcessName in~ (ScriptingHosts)
    | project DvcHostname, ActorUsername, TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessName
  ) on DvcHostname
| order by LongLabelQueries desc
ucs.rationale: DNS-tunnel C2 with long encoded subdomains and TXT-record bias is OilRig's defining channel across at least seven of their named families (Helminth, POWRUNER, BONDUPDATER, ALMA Communicator, DNSpionage, Glimpse, Saitama). The detection pivots on the *combination* APT34 actually uses — scripting-host or AppData-resident binary as the DNS originator + abnormally long first-label + TXT bias — rather

ucs.title: [LLM] APT34 IIS / Exchange web-shell (TwoFace, RGDoor, HighShell) — w3wp.exe spawning host recon
ucs.description: Catches OilRig's hands-on-keyboard phase after dropping a TwoFace/HighShell ASPX or RGDoor IIS module on a public-facing Exchange/OWA/IIS server: w3wp.exe (or umworkerprocess.exe) spawning the cmd/PowerShell recon trio they consistently run before lateral movement and Mimikatz.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count, values(Processes.process) as cmdlines, values(Processes.process_name) as children, dc(Processes.process_name) as unique_children, min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","hostname.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","route.exe","arp.exe","sc.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") AND Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name span=1h | rename Processes.* as * | where unique_children>=2 | search cmdlines="*whoami*" OR cmdlines="*net group*" OR cmdlines="*net user*" OR cmdlines="*nltest*" OR cmdlines="*ipconfig /all*" OR cmdlines="*systeminfo*" OR cmdlines="*tasklist*" OR cmdlines="*-enc*" OR cmdlines="*DownloadString*" OR cmdlines="*certutil -urlcache*" | table first_seen last_seen dest user parent_process_name unique_children children cmdlines
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let IISHosts = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe"]);
let ReconBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","hostname.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","route.exe","arp.exe","sc.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (IISHosts)
| where FileName in~ (ReconBins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service","iusr","defaultappool")
| extend ApppoolUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName
| summarize
    ChildCount     = count(),
    UniqueChildren = dcount(FileName),
    Children       = make_set(FileName, 16),
    SampleCmds     = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 16),
    FirstSeen      = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen       = max(Timestamp)
  by DeviceName, ApppoolUser, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where UniqueChildren >= 2
   or SampleCmds has_any ("whoami","net group","net user","nltest","ipconfig /all","systeminfo","tasklist","-enc ","-EncodedCommand","DownloadString","certutil -urlcache","bitsadmin /transfer")
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let IISHosts = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe"]);
let ReconBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","hostname.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","route.exe","arp.exe","sc.exe","reg.exe","wmic.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| extend ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    @"\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in~ (IISHosts)
| where ChildLeaf in~ (ReconBins)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where SubjectUserName !in~ ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE","IUSR")
| summarize
    ChildCount     = count(),
    UniqueChildren = dcount(ChildLeaf),
    Children       = make_set(ChildLeaf, 16),
    SampleCmds     = make_set(CommandLine, 16),
    FirstSeen      = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen       = max(TimeGenerated)
  by Computer, Account, ParentLeaf, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where UniqueChildren >= 2
   or SampleCmds has_any ("whoami","net group","net user","nltest","ipconfig /all","systeminfo","tasklist","-enc ","-EncodedCommand","DownloadString","certutil -urlcache","bitsadmin /transfer")
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT34 IIS Web Shell - w3wp.exe spawning host recon (TwoFace/RGDoor/HighShell)
id: 6b3a8d7f-2c4e-4b1a-9e1c-0d6a1e2f3b54
status: experimental
description: Detects post-exploitation activity following an APT34 (OilRig / Helix Kitten) ASPX web shell drop on Exchange/IIS, where w3wp.exe or umworkerprocess.exe spawn the standard OilRig recon set.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0049/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1087.002
  - attack.g0049
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\umworkerprocess.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Exchange health-monitoring scripts (rare; profile per environment)
  - Vulnerability scanners authenticated against IIS
level: high
ucs.rationale: OilRig has consistently dropped TwoFace, HighShell/HyperShell and the RGDoor IIS backdoor onto internet-facing Exchange/OWA throughout the FireEye 2017, Palo Alto Unit42 2018, and Mandiant 2020+ reporting; the post-shell recon set (whoami → nltest → net group 'domain admins' /domain → ipconfig /all → systeminfo → tasklist) is documented in their leaked operator playbook. The detection chains the r
